nodes.title: Bacterial SNPs in the human gut microbiome associate with host BMI

nodes.content: Discovered 1,358 statistically significant associations between bacterial SNPs and host BMI (Bonferroni-corrected P < 0.05), distilling to 40 independent associations after clumping
BMI-associated SNPs found in 27 bacterial species, with 12 showing no association by standard relative abundance analysis
17 associations replicated in a geographically independent cohort
Most associations were unexplained by diet, medications, or physical exercise
Revealed BMI association of SNP in inflammatory pathway of Bilophila wadsworthia and SNPs in energy metabolism functions in Faecalibacterium prausnitzii
Only examined associations with BMI as primary phenotype
Correlation threshold of 0.3 for clumping may affect independence of findings
Covariates limited to age, sex, and species relative abundance

nodes.title: Chechi 等 - 2026 - A gut microbiome-kidney-heart axis predictive of future cardiovascular diseases_副本

nodes.content: Gut microbiome-kidney-heart axis predicts future cardiovascular diseases
Multi-omics integration reveals interconnected pathways between gut microbiota, kidney function, and cardiovascular outcomes
Microbiome-based predictive models for cardiovascular disease risk stratification
Causality cannot be established from observational data
Potential confounding by diet and medication use affecting microbiome composition
Generalizability across different populations and ethnicities requires further validation

nodes.title: Reference-free Structural Variant Detection in Microbiomes via Long-read Coassembly Graphs

nodes.name: Development of rhea, a reference-free structural v

nodes.content: rhea outperforms existing methods for SV and horizontal gene transfer (HGT) detection
The method remains effective even when simulated reads diverge from reference genomes
Increased strain diversity does not compromise detection accuracy
Specific sequence alterations detected across environmental and fermented food microbiomes
Limited details on sample size and sequencing specifics
Generalizability to other microbiome types not fully explored

nodes.title: Mapping the gut microbial structural variations in healthy aging within the Chinese population

nodes.name: Shotgun metagenomic sequencing combined with compu
shotgun metagenomic sequencing (specific platform not specified)

nodes.content: 10,367 microbial structural variations (SVs) in 60 gut microbial species were identified from Chinese populations
There is a trend of copy number loss in age-related SVs during the human aging process, with 35 core SVs consistently detected
Eight specific microbial SVs can distinguish healthy from unhealthy aging, independent of age
A 3-kbp deletion SV of Bifidobacterium pseudocatenulatum, encoding plant polysaccharide degradation, is regulated by plant-based diet and contributes to healthy aging through bile acid metabolism
Microbial SVs harboring genes in methionine-homocysteine metabolism pathway are linked to chronic kidney disease (CKD)
Study focused exclusively on Chinese population, limiting generalizability to other ethnic groups
Cross-sectional design with limited longitudinal follow-up (only 615 participants followed up for 3 years)
Functional validation of identified SVs and their mechanistic links to healthy aging was not performed

nodes.name: Ultra-low coverage human genome imputation from sk

nodes.content: Geography explains more microbiome variation than skin type in the human skin microbiome
Host genetics contributes previously uncharacterized structure to the skin microbiome, exemplified by distinct profiles in Chinese individuals
Genome-wide association analysis identified 107 SNPs linked to 22 microbial taxa, including phages and plasmids, implicating host genes in skin structure, immunity, and lipid metabolism
Cutibacterium acnes and its phages exhibit geographic divergence and phage-host co-adaptation
Host-infecting viruses, particularly papillomaviruses, are associated with elevated microbial diversity and immune-modulatory functions
Host DNA contamination in skin samples makes shotgun metagenomics challenging
Sample size limited to 327 individuals across 5 countries may limit generalizability
Distinguishing viruses and plasmids from host genomes remains challenging with current classifiers

nodes.title: Microbiome-wide PheWAS links gut microbial SNVs to human health and exposures

nodes.content: Identified 37,945 associations with 237 host phenotypes and environmental exposures, including 3,018 associations supported by at least two cohorts for 105 host factors
Found 718 age-associated gut microbial SNVs across multiple cohorts, supporting variation in the gut microbiome as a hallmark of aging
Discovered 25 associations between microbial SNVs and diet in both Dutch and Tanzanian cohorts
Identified SNVs in an α-Melanocyte-stimulating hormone-mimicking clpB gene of Alistipes ihumii associated with host body weight and blood lipid levels
Found that SNVs of the isoleucyl-tRNA synthetase gene of Clostridium phoceensis mediate the beneficial impact of diet quality on host health
Limited population diversity despite inclusion of African cohort
Cross-sectional nature limits causal inference
Batch effects possible across cohorts

nodes.title: Host genetic regulation of human gut microbial structural variation

nodes.name: Meta-analysis of genetic associations between huma
metagenomic sequencing (specific platform not specified in truncated text)

nodes.content: Host genetics significantly regulates gut microbial structural variation through genome-wide associations between human genetic variants and bacterial metagenome variations
ABO and FUT2 genotypes jointly determine presence of a structural variation segment in Faecalibacterium prausnitzii containing an N-acetylgalactosamine (GalNAc) utilization gene cluster
GalNAc can be used as sole carbohydrate source by F. prausnitzii strains carrying the GalNAc-metabolizing pathway, with functional validation in vitro
Other ABO-associated species including Collinsella aerofaciens can also utilize GalNAc
GalNAc utilization genes are associated with host cardiometabolic health, particularly in individuals with mucosal A-antigen
Population-specific: discovery in Dutch cohorts with limited replication in Tanzanian cohort
Cross-sectional design limits causal inference for host-microbiome interactions
Mechanistic details of how ABO/FUT2 genotypes shape intestinal environment remain to be fully elucidated

nodes.title: Structural variation in the gut microbiome associates with host health

nodes.name: Systematic identification of microbial genomic str

nodes.content: Structural variants (SVs) are highly prevalent in the human gut microbiome across bacterial phyla and replicate across independent cohorts (76% of SVs replicated in Dutch cohort)
SVs are enriched for CRISPR-associated and antibiotic-producing functions but depleted from housekeeping genes, suggesting a role in microbial adaptation
124 significant associations were found between SVs and host disease risk factors, with 40 associations replicating in the independent Dutch cohort
A genomic region in Anaerostipes hadrus encoding a composite inositol catabolism-butyrate biosynthesis pathway was identified, with presence associated with lower host metabolic disease risk
SVs represent a nascent layer of variability in the microbiome associated with microbial adaptation and host health
Reference database dependency: reliance on 3,953 bacterial genomes may miss novel SVs from uncharacterized species
Algorithm limitation: ICRA may have reduced sensitivity for very rare or highly divergent strains
Population specificity: significant SV-disease associations may be specific to particular populations and not universally generalizable

nodes.title: Gut microbial structural variation associates with immune checkpoint inhibitor response

nodes.name: Metagenome-wide association study analyzing gut mi

nodes.content: Microbial genomic structural variants (SVs) in gut bacteria are associated with immune checkpoint inhibitor (ICI) treatment response
Associated SVs are located in multiple species including Akkermansia muciniphila, Dorea formicigenerans, and Bacteroides caccae
Genes encoding enzymes involved in glucose metabolism are harbored in SV regions associated with ICI response
This work identifies a nascent layer of gut microbiome heterogeneity that correlates with host prognosis following ICI treatment
Full limitations section not visible in truncated text
Potential confounding factors from different cancer types across cohorts
Causality cannot be established from association study alone

nodes.title: Rural–urban environmental differences shape gut microbiome and metabolome signatures linked to cardiovascular disease risk in sub-Saharan Africa

nodes.content: Rural individuals had significantly higher gut microbial alpha diversity and distinct beta diversity compared to urban residents (Shannon index p = 9.17 x 10^-12; Bray-Curtis PERMANOVA p < 0.001)
250 out of 1008 microbial taxa differed in abundance between settings, with Succinivibrio and Desulfovibrio urban-enriched and Agathobacter and Faecalibacterium rural-enriched
Metabolomic profiling revealed distinct setting-specific signatures with prenol lipids, fatty acyls, and carboxylic acids predominant among differentially abundant metabolites
Vitamin K metabolism was identified as a major biological pathway enriched by differentially abundant metabolites
Several taxa such as Succinivibrio and Erysipelatoclostridiaceae mediated the association between urban living and elevated blood pressure
Cross-sectional design cannot establish causality between microbiome changes and CVD risk
Study conducted within a single country (Uganda) limiting generalizability to other sub-Saharan African populations
Specific bioinformatics pipeline details, software versions, and database versions not reported

nodes.title: Characterization of gut microbial structural variations as determinants of human bile acid metabolism

nodes.content: Characterized 8,282 structural variants (SVs) across 55 bacterial species in gut microbiomes of 1,437 individuals
Identified 809 replicable associations between bacterial SVs and bile acid parameters through metagenome-wide association analysis
Both SV-based continuous genetic makeup and discrete clusters showed correlations with bile acid metabolism
SV regulators mediate the effects of lifestyle factors on bile acid metabolism
SV-based metagenome analysis enables discovery of previously unknown bile acid transformation genes
Observational study design cannot establish causality between microbial SVs and bile acid metabolism
A considerable proportion of BA-related genes are still unknown and not captured by current analysis
Study limited to fasting plasma samples; postprandial BA dynamics not assessed

nodes.title: Dissecting multi-gene virulence phenotypes with base editing requires consideration of target-independent mutagenesis

nodes.name: CRISPR base editing was employed using temperature

nodes.content: Multiplexed base editing can reproducibly achieve simultaneous editing of three target genes and can be iteratively extended to produce complex multi-gene knockouts in Salmonella Typhimurium
A base-edited multi-knockout strain showed reduced virulence in vivo similar to a traditionally constructed recombineering mutant
Off-target cytidine deaminase activity was detected via whole-genome sequencing, producing variable virulence phenotypes in vivo in a clone-dependent manner
Statistical power analysis can predict how many edited mutants are needed to confidently measure fitness functions accounting for off-target mutations
Target-independent mutagenesis confounds accurate discovery of genetic interactions in bacterial pathogens
Target-independent cytidine deaminase activity produces off-target mutations in bacterial chromosomes
Variable levels of off-target editing lead to clone-dependent virulence phenotypes
Off-target mutations may confound accurate discovery of genetic interactions
Phylogenetic differences in 85% of species were associated with exploratory behavior (FDR<0.05)
146 million microbial SNVs were associated with 953 plasma metabolites, identifying 34 study-wide significant associations (P<2.9×10^-8)
4-Anisic acid and D-galacturonate were identified as key metabolites linking microbial SNVs to host cognitive exploration via structural regulation of membrane transporters
3,548 novel species-level genomes were identified compared to existing sheep databases, from a total of 5,253 species-level MAGs generated
Microbial genetic variation plays a critical role in modulating host neurocognition through metabolite regulation
Study limited to Merino sheep breed with controlled conditions; findings may not generalize to other species or human populations
Specific sequencing depth and bioinformatics pipeline details not fully reported
Causal mechanisms of SNV-driven metabolite regulation on neurocognition require further experimental validation

nodes.title: Bai 等 - 2025 - Microbial genetic composition regulates host social behavior_副本

nodes.name: High-throughput screening of a genome-wide single-

nodes.content: Screened 3,983 E. coli knockout strains and identified 370 mutant strains that significantly reduced social aggregation behavior in C. elegans
Five mutations in the L-tyrosine biosynthesis pathway significantly alter social aggregation behavior of C. elegans via the TGF-β signaling pathway
Tyrosine was significantly enriched among differentially abundant metabolites associated with reduced social behavior
Decreased tyrosine synthesis in E. coli was identified as crucial for reduced worm aggregation behavior through GO and KEGG pathway analyses
Model organism limitations: C. elegans is a simplified model that may not fully replicate complex mammalian gut-brain interactions
Single bacterial species: Study used only E. coli mutants, whereas natural gut microbiota comprises complex communities
Mechanistic details not fully elucidated: TGF-β pathway involvement identified but detailed molecular mechanisms require further investigation
